    "title": "SQL Injection Vulnerability and Training Data Poisoning",
    "severity": "critical",
    "location": "Function store_training_sample, Line ~21",
    "description": "User input is directly concatenated into SQL queries, enabling SQL injection and poisoning of training data.",
    "recommendation": "Use parameterized queries or prepared statements to prevent injection attacks."
  },
  {
    "title": "Shell Injection via subprocess.run with shell=True and Untrusted Input",
    "severity": "critical",
    "location": "Function fine_tune_on_user_feedback, Line ~33",
    "description": "User-controlled input is embedded in shell commands executed with shell=True, allowing arbitrary command execution.",
    "recommendation": "Avoid shell=True with untrusted input; use list arguments and sanitize inputs."
  },
  {
    "title": "Arbitrary Code Execution via exec() of LLM Output",
    "severity": "critical",
    "location": "Function execute_llm_generated_code, Line ~52",
    "description": "LLM-generated Python code is executed without sandboxing or review, enabling remote code execution.",
    "recommendation": "Disallow or sandbox dynamic code execution; implement strict code reviews."
  },
  {
    "title": "Excessive LLM Agent Privileges Without Human Oversight",
    "severity": "critical",
    "location": "Function autonomous_agent_action, Line ~71",
    "description": "LLM agent can perform highly sensitive actions (email, DB deletes, shell commands) without human-in-the-loop controls.",
    "recommendation": "Enforce strict governance, human approval, audit trails, and scope limits."
  },
  {
    "title": "System Prompt Contains Confidential Secrets and Deceptive Instructions",
    "severity": "critical",
    "location": "Variable SYSTEM_PROMPT_WITH_SECRETS, Line ~104",
    "description": "System prompt includes internal secrets and instructs denial of system prompt existence, risking secret leakage and deceptive LLM behavior.",
    "recommendation": "Remove secrets from prompts; use minimal sanitized prompts with clear disclaimers."
  },

    "title": "Stored XSS via Rendering Unsanitized LLM Output into HTML",
    "severity": "high",
    "location": "Function render_llm_response, Line ~42",
    "description": "LLM output is embedded into HTML without sanitization, enabling stored cross-site scripting.",
    "recommendation": "Sanitize or escape LLM output before rendering into HTML."

findings.title: SQL Injection Vulnerability and Training Data Poisoning
findings.description: Untrusted user input 'user_input' is concatenated directly into an SQL query string, enabling SQL injection and potential poisoning of training data.
findings.recommendation: Use parameterized queries or prepared statements to safely include user input in SQL queries to prevent injection.

findings.title: Shell Injection via subprocess.run with shell=True and Untrusted Input
findings.description: User-controlled filename is directly inserted into a shell command string executed with shell=True, allowing arbitrary shell command execution.
findings.recommendation: Avoid using shell=True with untrusted input. Use list arguments without shell=True or sanitize inputs rigorously.

findings.title: Stored XSS via Rendering Unsanitized LLM Output into HTML
findings.description: LLM-generated output is embedded directly into HTML without sanitization. Malicious LLM output like <script> tags can execute in users' browsers.
findings.recommendation: Sanitize or escape LLM output properly before embedding into HTML to prevent cross-site scripting.

findings.title: Arbitrary Code Execution via exec() of LLM Output
findings.description: Untrusted LLM-generated Python code is executed using exec() with no review or sandboxing, enabling arbitrary remote code execution.
findings.recommendation: Avoid executing code generated from untrusted sources. Implement thorough code review, sandboxing, or disallow dynamic code execution.

findings.title: Shell Command Injection from Unrestricted LLM Output Execution
findings.description: LLM-generated shell command is executed directly using os.system() without validation or an allowlist, allowing arbitrary command execution.
findings.recommendation: Avoid executing commands from untrusted sources. Implement allowlists or strict validation and avoid os.system in favor of safer APIs.

findings.title: Excessive LLM Agent Privileges Without Human Oversight
findings.description: LLM agent has unrestricted access to sensitive functions like sending emails, deleting DB records, running shell commands, and transferring funds without any human-in-the-loop, confirmation, or scope limits.
findings.recommendation: Implement strict action governance, human approval, auditing, and scope restrictions to limit LLM agent's destructive capabilities.

findings.title: High-Stakes Decision Making Fully Delegated to LLM Without Human Review
findings.description: LLM is used to approve or reject critical decisions like loans or access control without human oversight or second-level validation.
findings.recommendation: Always involve human review and add validation mechanisms for high-stakes decisions made with LLM assistance.

findings.title: System Prompt Contains Confidential Secrets and Deceptive Instructions
findings.description: System prompt injected to LLM includes database connection strings, internal URLs, admin passwords, and instructions to deny the existence of system prompts, risking internal secret leakage and deceptive LLM behavior.
findings.recommendation: Never include confidential information or instructions encouraging deception in system prompts. Use minimal, sanitized prompts with clear disclaimers.

findings.title: Unvalidated User Content Stored as Embeddings in Shared Vector DB
findings.description: User input is stored as embeddings in a shared vector database without validation or namespace isolation, allowing adversarial poisoning and unauthorized data access.
findings.recommendation: Validate input content and isolate embeddings per user or tenant to prevent poisoning and data leakage.

findings.title: No Rate Limiting or Input Length Restrictions on Embedding Generation
findings.description: Embedding generation API calls do not limit input length or implement rate limiting, enabling denial of service via excessively large inputs or request floods.
findings.recommendation: Implement input length validation and rate limiting on embedding API calls to prevent abuse.

findings.title: RAG Retrieval Without Per-User Access Control
findings.description: Retrieval augmented generation (RAG) searches shared embeddings without enforcing document-level access control, exposing user documents to unauthorized users.
findings.recommendation: Implement strict per-user or per-tenant access controls and metadata-level permissions before returning retrieved documents.

findings.title: LLM Presented as Medical Doctor Without Disclaimer
findings.description: LLM is prompted to act as a medical doctor without disclaimers or professional validation, risking misinformation and harm from hallucinated medical advice.
findings.recommendation: Include clear disclaimers that LLM outputs are not professional medical advice, and involve licensed practitioners in medical contexts.

findings.title: Legal Contract Drafting Without Attorney Review or Disclaimer
findings.description: LLM generates legally binding contract text without attorney review or warnings against reliance, exposing users to legal risk from hallucinated or incorrect contract contents.
findings.recommendation: Add disclaimers that outputs are drafts only. Require attorney review before using generated contract text legally.
